compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Plan-review remediation — final targeted round on the S2/S4/S5 residual (the inconsistencies the (b) edits introduced). Applied:

- **S2 (0ee8):** dropped the stale "`autolander-bot` vote ACL / bot-only ACL" cross-ref. S1 grants the `Autosubmit` vote to group **Contributors (requesters)** so *agents* set the label via S4's `land`; the loop itself casts no votes. Re-worded the S1 dependency to "requester-votable (Contributor) ACL" and clarified the loop only *selects on* `label:Autosubmit+1`.
- **REST-submittable vs FFO-landable (S2 + S4):** added an explicit definition — *REST-submittable* = `submittable=True` (gate votes MAX); *FFO-landable* = also on `main`'s current tip. Under FFO (ADR-0040) they diverge (a behind-tip change stays `submittable=True` but `POST /submit` fails `not fast-forward`). Corrected S2's TOCTOU guard to re-check **FFO-landable** (tip-parent), not just `is:submittable`; framed S4's `needs_rebase` as "REST-submittable but not FFO-landable."
- **S4→S5 heartbeat dependency (S4 + S5):** S4 read the heartbeat "(from S5)" without declaring it. Added an explicit **interface dependency on S5** in S4's Dependencies — a heartbeat-*contract* dependency (agreed location + staleness threshold), NOT a build-order blocker. Tightened S5 AC5 to name that heartbeat as the interface contract S4's `lander_down` reads.
- **Bogus CONTRIBUTING §FFO citation (S4):** there is no "§FFO" section. Repointed the `lander_down` message to **CONTRIBUTING §2e ("Submit"), ADR-0040** — the actual home of the manual FFO rebase-and-submit steps.
- **S4→ADR-0042 downstream forward-reference:** removed. ADR-0042 is authored downstream by S6; S4 no longer cites a doc that does not yet exist. `land --help` now cites only `docs/land-contract.md`; the ADR↔contract linkage stays in S6.
- **S5 existing-file edit targets (3c55):** added a `## Files` section (new vs edited) and expanded `file_impact` to the full set — new: `Dockerfile.autolander`, `monitoring_autolander.tf`; edited existing: `docker-compose.yml`, `terraform/ssm.tf`, `scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`, `scripts/observability.sh`. Noted `iam.tf` as read-only (already grants the SSM prefix) and the `All-Projects refs/meta/config` capability grant as an operator cutover, not a repo file.

**Declined (over-specification):** did NOT pin an exact heartbeat staleness threshold (seconds) / poll interval or fix the AC6 queue-wait histogram bucket boundaries. These are implementer decisions; the plan should carry the *contract* (an agreed location + a staleness threshold, a histogram metric) and let the implementation choose the concrete numbers — over-pinning them here reduces flexibility without adding review value. Kept "a few poll intervals" / "bounded" as written.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Follow-up — retracting the earlier "declined over-specification." Per reviewer direction, the poll interval and staleness threshold are design-time judgement calls that must be fixed here (a less-capable implementation agent should not have to invent them). Now pinned, consistently across S2/S4/S5:

- **Bot poll interval = 15 s** (S2, the loop cadence): picks up a freshly-`Autosubmit`'d change within ~15 s while staying gentle on the Gerrit REST API; intentionally distinct from `land`'s 30 s client-side poll (S4).
- **Heartbeat cadence = every poll (≤ 15 s)** (S5 AC5), tied to S2's 15 s loop.
- **`lander_down` staleness threshold = 90 s** (S4) = 6× the 15 s poll: absorbs a few consecutive slow iterations / a transient Gerrit hiccup without a false positive, yet fires ~20× sooner than the 30-min `--wait` timeout.
- **AC6 time-to-land histogram buckets = 30 s, 1 m, 2 m, 5 m, 10 m, 20 m, 30 m, 1 h** (S5): spans a green fast-forward landing (~1–2 min CI re-run) through a contended `N=5` re-drive, so p50/p90/p99 are readable.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Correction (re-review round). The re-run flagged that my "REST-submittable vs FFO-landable" disambiguation had the mechanics INVERTED, contradicting ADR-0040. Verified against ADR-0040 (§ "submittable only if a descendant of the current main tip … the change becomes non-submittable until rebased"): under FFO, Gerrit's `submittable` ALREADY requires descendant-of-tip, so a behind-tip change is **non-submittable** (`submittable=false`), NOT `submittable=True`. Reframed S2 + S4 accordingly:
- The real distinctions are (1) a TOCTOU race — `submittable=True` is only true as of the read, so re-confirm just before `POST /submit` (FFO refuses a stale submit with "not fast-forward"); and (2) a native `submittable=false` doesn't say WHY (behind-tip → `needs_rebase` vs a failing vote → `ci_failed`/`review_failed`), which is why `needs_rebase` needs the bot's S3 marker.
- Dropped the misleading "REST-submittable / FFO-landable" terminology.

NOTE for reviewer: the re-review at the new head SHA surfaced that S2/S4/S5 still BLOCK on a substantial set of PRE-EXISTING design gaps well beyond this residual round (e.g. `/rebase:chain` is not a real Gerrit REST path; the `N=5` "park" mechanism is undefined; multi-member chain needs per-member Verified SHAs not a single `tested_sha`; CloudWatch has no native histogram-with-buckets; `autoheal` vs `restart: unless-stopped` for unhealthy; the autolander state volume + compose-up.sh SITE_SUBDIRS drift gate). The epic itself PASSES and is signed; the children do not. These are real design decisions, not wording — flagged for a scope decision rather than silently resolved here.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Mechanical remediation pass (non-design findings) applied to S2/S4/S5. Highlights:

- **REJECTED as invalid — S2 BLOCK T1** ("`/rebase:chain` is not a real Gerrit endpoint"). Verified against the Gerrit REST API docs: `POST /changes/{change-id}/rebase:chain` DOES exist (3.6+, so the 3.14.1 instance has it) — the review's own advisory T3 concedes this. Kept the endpoint; wrote the precise path. The fact-check DID surface a real fix: the RebaseInput field is **`rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader`**, not `on_behalf_of_uploader`.
- **S2:** per-member SHA tracking (`tested_shas{}`) + all-members-fresh-Verified rule for chains (R5 safety); `re_drive_count` + `paused` added to the schema/phase enum; ancestor-atomic submit = `POST /changes/{tip}/submit`; chain FFO check = bottom-most member's parent == main tip; FIFO field = `ApprovalInfo.date` via `o=DETAILED_LABELS`; bounded Verified-await; Submit-ACL + impersonation-capability + credential preconditions (xref S5); named `infra/autolander/loop.py`; testing AC.
- **S4:** heartbeat checked BEFORE setting `Autosubmit` (no orphaned label) + heartbeat-read-failure → `lander_down`; `o=DETAILED_LABELS` for vote reads; ALL exit codes pinned (`4 not_requested`, `7 error`, `75 pending`); land-status fallback-precedence + never-requested behavior; JSON schema added to `docs/land-contract.md`; named `infra/autolander/land.py`; testing AC.
- **S5:** declared the external state volume + `compose-up.sh SITE_SUBDIRS` edit (incident-2731 drift gate); factual fix — container `unhealthy` is a Docker event, not a journald line (probe via `docker inspect`); HEALTHCHECK probes the LOCAL heartbeat (never Gerrit, avoids flap); `ssm.tf` for_each + `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` into shared `.env`; All-Projects `[capability]` provisioning named; bounded SIGTERM drain; scope reconciled (service-lifecycle wiring vs core loop); E2E proving step + testing AC.

REMAINING: 7 genuine design decisions surfaced to the reviewer one at a time (heartbeat form; park/hand-back protocol + S2/S3 boundary; CloudWatch histogram transport; unhealthy→restart mechanism; land Gerrit-client reuse; agent credential injection; queue-status transport + emergency-stop sentinel form). Not decided unilaterally.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Prior-art research (Zuul, LUCI CV/Chromium CQ, Gerrit plugins, Prow/Tide) resolved 6 of 7 design decisions by strong convergence (per reviewer approval to adopt convergent prior art):

- **D1 liveness/status surface** → CQ pattern: bot posts per-change outcome BACK to the Gerrit change (comment + `Autosubmit` label state), which `land` reads via the Gerrit creds it already holds; PLUS a Zuul/Tide-style **read-only HTTP status endpoint** for bot liveness (heartbeat age) + queue-status (AC6). Resolves the "file vs metric" ambiguity: local heartbeat file backs the in-container HEALTHCHECK; the status endpoint surfaces it to remote `land`.
- **D2 failed-rebase hand-back** → CQ "reset": on conflict / post-rebase `Verified -1` / N=5 exhaustion, the bot REMOVES `Autosubmit` + posts a comment naming the reason + writes S3's needs_rebase marker. Label-removal is the "handed back" signal remote `land` detects. This concretizes the previously-undefined S2 "park" mechanism.
- **D3 time-to-land metric** → Zuul: emit a RAW queue-wait/resident_time timer; percentiles/buckets computed DOWNSTREAM (CloudWatch extended statistics), NOT hard-coded in the emitter. Dropped the fixed histogram buckets I had pinned.
- **D5 Gerrit client** → reuse **pygerrit2** (thin wrapper) in the autolander tooling; do NOT reimplement and do NOT import `src/rebar`. Resolves the client-reuse BLOCK.
- **D6 credential injection** → `land` uses the agent's ambient Gerrit HTTP credential (`.netrc` / git credential helper, via pygerrit2 HTTPBasicAuthFromNetrc); the bot uses RebarBotNava's SSM-backed HTTP password + `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` (keeps the human as uploader, forces CI re-verify).
- **D7 emergency-stop + drain** → Zuul pause/graceful: a stop-consuming sentinel (state-volume file, checked each loop) → `paused`; graceful drain finishes the in-flight wipChain + writes its S3 outcome before exit.

REMAINING (diverges — surfaced to reviewer): **D4** the plain-docker-compose unhealthy→restart mechanism (prior art is K8s liveness probes; not directly applicable).

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: D4 resolved (reviewer decision): plain-docker-compose hang-recovery uses the willfarrell/autoheal sidecar — HEALTHCHECK marks the container unhealthy on a stale local heartbeat; autoheal (watching Docker health state, socket mounted read-only) restarts the autoheal=true-labeled container. Noted as a new stack dependency + Docker-socket attack-surface item for the deploy review. All 7 design decisions now resolved; re-running plan review on epic + S2/S4/S5.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: D5 resolved (thin stdlib helper in infra/autolander). Rationale: the no-import rule is THIS epic's load-bearing decision (keep rebar core platform-agnostic; defer the Gerrit/GitHub/GitLab provider-seam to idea 1701). Peers (Zuul drivers, LUCI/CV vendoring go-gerrit) extract-and-share eventually, which == the deferred seam; but the existing src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py (stdlib-only, no rebase/submit surface) is not a drop-in, and a production review_bot refactor mid-feature is out-of-scope risk. So a thin stdlib helper mirroring the proven urllib/Basic-auth/XSSI pattern is the low-risk fit; unification behind the provider-seam happens when 1701 is built. Reverses cleanly to a shared-module extraction if desired. All 7 design decisions now resolved + pygerrit2 premise removed + /rebase:chain rejected as invalid (verified against the live instance's own docs). Re-running plan review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Round-4 — incorporated reviewer feedback (maintainability + reliability + rationale capture):

- **Sub-decomposed the heavy stories.** S2 → tasks S2a (89f9 selection+rebase-routing+stdlib Gerrit helper), S2b (a9e3 wipChain+TOCTOU+hand-back), S2c (1ccf fresh-Verified-per-member+atomic-submit). S5 → tasks S5a (08e1 container+compose+flock+volume), S5b (ea61 credentials+All-Projects capability), S5c (9a6f observability+status-endpoint), S5d (38be liveness+heartbeat+emergency-stop+drain+crash-recovery). Story-level AC trimmed to "what/why" with pointers to children; each task is now loadable whole (module-size ethos). S4 left intact (coherent, already signed).
- **Reliability fallback (accepted):** S5d adds a CONCRETE crash-safe recovery record (`recovery.json` snapshot in the state volume) reconciled against live Gerrit + S3 markers on restart (no double-submit, no stranded stack) + an explicit crash-recovery/idempotency TEST that kills the bot at each phase. This also clears S5's round-3 BLOCK on the underspecified SIGTERM recovery contract.
- **High-visibility failures (operability):** S5c makes hangs/errors/stuck-states loud — `autolander_errors` from three feeders (AUTOLANDER_ERROR marker on ANY unhandled error + hand-back, docker-inspect unhealthy, lander_down), a named `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` metric (clears the unnamed-metric BLOCK), and a stuck-state time-in-phase alarm.
- **Provisioning fork resolved:** All-Projects capability grant committed to a concrete new file `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh` (clears S5's fork BLOCKs); `loop.py` now appears in child Files (clears the COH BLOCK).
- **Rationale captured in ADR-0042:** full decision record drafted now (attached to S6) — the 8 key decisions + prior-art grounding + R5 invariant + separation-of-concerns + reliability tradeoff + the DEFERRAL TRIGGERS for the future idea-epics (1701 provider-seam, c3a9 bounded-speculation batching, HA). S6's AC updated to require landing exactly that.

Round-3 state before this: epic/S2/S4 PASS+signed; S5 blocked (7). Re-running review on the trimmed S2/S5, S6, and the 7 new tasks.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Task-level review triage (round-4 verdicts: stories S2/S5/S6 + task S2b PASS+signed; 6 new tasks blocked). Fixed after fact-checking against repo config + the live instance docs:
- S5b (biggest correction): the review was RIGHT — my All-Projects capability+submit design was wrong. project.config sets exclusiveGroupPermissions=submit (eclipses inherited All-Projects submit), and RebarBotNava is already in Contributors (holds Submit). And rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader is NOT a global [capability] — the Rebase access right is a per-ref ACL (confirmed in the instance access-control.html). Reworked S5b: drop the All-Projects script + submit grant; add the Rebase access right to the rebar project's refs/meta/config via setup-project.sh; verify Contributors membership. Fixed the S5 story pointer to match.
- S2c: corrected ancestor-atomic submit to Gerrit 'Submitted Together' (relation-chain ancestry, reinforced by S3's assigned topic + active submitWholeTopic) AND added the review's valuable partial-land safety check (verify ALL members MERGED; loud error + hand-back otherwise).
- S5c: fixed the alarm data-flow (CloudWatch alarms watch a published time_in_phase metric, not HTTP JSON) + added an AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK marker (lander_down is client-side) + concrete metric-shape proving test.
- S5d: recovery rules now cover ALL four kill-point phases (rebasing/awaiting_verified → discard+re-select idempotently; submitting → Gerrit-check; handback → complete idempotent removal).
- S2a: uploader/trailer-preservation test; S5a: autoheal-sidecar AC.
Re-reviewing the 6 tasks + S5.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Task triage round-2 (S2a/S2c/S5c now PASS+signed). Fixed the finer-grained blocks by pinning concrete values (per 'fully clarify now'):
- REAL bug caught: the autoheal Docker socket must be mounted READ-WRITE (autoheal issues docker restart, a daemon write); my :ro would silently break restart. Corrected in S5a/S5d/S5.
- S5a: pinned the volume name gerrit_autolander (via compose-up.sh gerrit_ prefix helper), the lockfile autolander.lock, and a concrete flock proving command.
- S5b: resolved the rebase-on-behalf ACL concretely (no more 'verify at impl') — [access refs/heads/*] rebase = group Contributors in the rebar project.config; the on-behalf half needs no extra grant (checks the original uploader's existing push right); get_param (fail-fast); synthetic scratch-ref smoke change; named fallback on 403.
- S5c (already passed): metric-based stuck alarm + AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK marker.
- S5d: drain bounded to 120s + compose stop_grace_period: 120s (Docker's 10s default would SIGKILL before recovery.json is written); recovery rules for all 4 phases.
- S5 story: pinned the stuck threshold (>45 min in a non-idle phase) + socket-rw note.
Re-reviewing S5 + S5a/b/d.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Task triage round-3 (S5a now PASS+signed). Two sharp catches fixed:
- **Drain⇄autoheal race (real design flaw):** during a 120s drain the bot stops heartbeating → HEALTHCHECK (~90s) fires → autoheal docker restart (which does NOT honour stop_grace_period) could kill the bot before recovery.json is written. Fixed in S5d: recovery.json is written as the FIRST SIGTERM action (so any interruption is safe) AND the bot keeps heartbeating during drain (so autoheal isn't provoked) — belt-and-suspenders; added a test case asserting the race is benign.
- **S5b smoke-test self-contradiction:** a MERGED change can't be abandoned. Fixed: the smoke test runs entirely on an isolated scratch-autolander branch (submit merges there harmlessly, no post-merge abandon), with a concrete jq uploader-preserved assertion and a new smoke-check-autolander.sh file.
- Reframed 'mid-handback' as an S3-marker condition (acknowledged=false), NOT a wipChain phase value (clears S5's enum-mismatch block without touching S2b's signed enum).
Re-reviewing S5 + S5b + S5d.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Plan previously signed CLEAN (blocking:0) at 2272d68; only main drifted (no plan change). Re-review BLOCK is a verifiable false positive: the snapshot-capture AC IS present (names S1 as actor, infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json as store, before-cutover timing) and the block contradicts the review's own advisory that 'parent explicitly assigns capture to S1'. Valid advisories (ci_failed provenance, failure-handling consistency) remediated; rest non-actionable/impact-0.0/already-fixed. Full per-finding triage in session log 547b.".

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: STRATEGIC PIVOT (2026-07-13): the serial auto-lander this epic delivered is being RETIRED. After a sustained bug spiral (dc33/15a1/0d3e) + the FFO re-CI cost, the owner decided the client-side rebase bridge is too expensive to maintain for a POC. Superseded by epic d4de (fleshy-kimberlite-crayfish): switch main FFO->Rebase-If-Necessary so Gerrit integrates server-side, drop the bot, agents Submit directly, post-merge main CI + manual revert as the bounded-risk safety net. The delivered stories (S1-S6) remain closed (work was real); the ARCHITECTURE is superseded. See ADR 0047.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

Parallel agent sessions race to land changes on the Fast-Forward-Only `main` (ADR-0040).
The loser of each race must manually rebase-to-tip and retry, and with a large feature
**stack** this update-and-retry causes many CI re-runs on non-conflicting changes — burning
**wall-clock** (GitHub Actions minutes are free for this OSS repo, but concurrency is capped)
and **agent tokens/attention** (sessions hand-roll the rebase + poll for submittability).

This epic builds the **auto-lander**: agents set an opt-in intent and walk away; changes land
autonomously (or are handed back with a single actionable outcome), while the strong two-vote
gate is never weakened.

> **Terminology.** "stack" (the agent's view of a related series) and "chain" (a Gerrit
> relation chain) are used synonymously throughout this epic and its children.

**Separation-of-concerns scope (the load-bearing decision):** landing/merge-queueing is
**platform-specific and maintainer-owned**, NOT part of rebar's platform-agnostic
ticket/workflow/gate/reconcile mission. So everything here is **THIS project's infrastructure**
(Gerrit + GitHub Actions), living in `infra/` + a project CLI/script — it MAY `import rebar` for
ticket ops (e.g. close a ticket on merge) but adds **no Gerrit-specific landing logic to
`src/rebar` core**. The platform-agnostic generalization is deliberately deferred to a separate
`idea` (the landing provider-seam, `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`).

**Design settled in the brainstorm (all grounded in OSS prior art):**
- **Phase 1 = serial lander (this epic's committed scope).** Land the front `Autosubmit`
  change/chain: rebase to tip → await fresh Verified → ancestor-atomic submit.
- **Phase 2 = throughput, DEFERRED.** Cross-stack batching was evaluated by a design spike and
  **rejected** on this infra (the gerrit-to-platform bridge fires CI per-patchset → N runs not one;
  landing N off one run needs a GerriScary-class Verified direct-cast; wins only in rare bursts of
  ≥4 + a bisection tax + WIP-hook fragility). The recommended throughput approach — **bounded
  speculation** (speculate ≤ the GHA concurrency cap; each candidate a clean single-change-on-tip
  verified on its own tree) — is deferred to idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`, promoted only if the serial
  treadmill becomes a MEASURED pain.
- **Gate substrate stays:** FFO as the R5 backstop; strict `Verified` copyCondition
  (drops on rebase → CI re-runs); LLM-Review carries `TRIVIAL_REBASE` (ADR-0041) so a
  conflict-free rebase re-runs ONLY CI, not the LLM review.
- **`Autosubmit` opt-in label = a lease the agent hands the bot;** coexists with manual submit.
- **The stack is the atomic unit for landing AND failure:** never partial-land, never evict a
  change from *within* a stack; on failure the **whole stack is handed back to its owning agent**
  to remediate (Goal-2 exception: agent is pulled in only when code must change).
- **Agent contract = one command → one typed outcome** (`merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed |
  review_failed`); the `land` command is the single point that *returns* the outcome, so agents
  never correlate votes/labels/status themselves. Delivered as project tooling reading Gerrit
  primitives. **Outcome provenance (the deliberate split model — NOT co-located; this is the
  authoritative statement, superseding any looser "single source of truth" phrasing elsewhere):**
  the one outcome the bot must *decide and persist* — `needs_rebase` (a rebase conflict /
  FFO-not-landable state, which is NOT a native Gerrit vote and so has no live signal) — is
  **recorded by the lander into its own state (S3)** and read back by `land`; the three outcomes
  that ARE native Gerrit facts — `merged` (change status MERGED), `ci_failed` (a post-rebase
  `Verified -1` on the current patchset) and `review_failed` (an `LLM-Review -1`) — are **derived
  live by the `land` command from Gerrit primitives (S3/S4)**, not stored by the bot. (This
  matches S3 AC3 + S4 AC5, which own the code: only `needs_rebase` is persisted; `ci_failed` is
  read live from the current `Verified` vote.) So `land` is the single *reporter* of a unified
  typed outcome, composed from one bot-state read (`needs_rebase`) + live Gerrit reads.

## Greenfield note

This epic is greenfield: the `Autosubmit` label, the autolander service + its `wipChain` and state dir, the `land`/`land-status` command + outcome enum, the outcome-record schema, and ADR-0042 are all NEW artifacts created by this epic's stories. Cross-story references to them are forward-references to sibling deliverables (see each story's `## Dependencies`), not references to existing code.

## Success Criteria

- An agent sets the landing intent and walks away; in the common (non-conflicting) case the
  change/stack lands with **zero further agent attention** and **no manual rebase-retry**.
- On any failure the agent receives **one typed, actionable outcome** — it never watches votes or
  correlates multiple Gerrit primitives, and the "both votes green but conflicts" trap is handled
  inside the tool, not by the agent.
- **Nothing lands whose to-be-merged tree wasn't CI-tested** (FFO + strict Verified preserved;
  two-vote gate never weakened — GerriScary posture audited).
- Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated (each change/stack is CI'd only
  as needed by the serial lander). Throughput beyond serial is a deferred Phase-2 concern (bounded
  speculation, idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`), not a committed success criterion here.
- **No Gerrit-specific landing logic in `src/rebar` core** — rebar stays platform-agnostic.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Phase 1 — serial auto-lander (first deliverable)
- [ ] `Autosubmit` Gerrit label authored in `infra/gerrit/project.config` / pushed to
      `refs/meta/config`: **requester-votable ACL** (vote granted to group `Contributors`, so an
      agent sets the label under its OWN identity via the land command — the bot casts no votes;
      the grant is tightly scoped, not a bot-only or open grant), **non-gating** (informational;
      gating is redundant under FFO),
      **non-sticky** (empty copyCondition).
- [ ] Serial auto-lander service (project infra; deploys like the review-bot —
      `infra/compose/Dockerfile.*` + compose): for the front `Autosubmit`+submittable change/chain
      it rebases to the current tip (`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`,
      `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers) → awaits a fresh
      `Verified +1` → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA. Single `wipChain` (serialized,
      one instance).
- [ ] **No gate-semantics change** — the serial lander reads existing `Verified` votes and casts
      NO votes (the bot never casts Verified in this epic's committed scope).
- [ ] Failure handling: CI-fail → remove `Autosubmit`, rely on the native `Verified -1` + run
      logs; rebase-conflict (`409`) → remove `Autosubmit` and record a conflict outcome. **Never
      partial-land; never evict a change from within a stack** — the whole stack is handed back to
      its owning agent.
- [ ] **Agent landing contract (the lead story):** a single **project** command that requests a
      land and returns ONE typed terminal outcome (`merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed |
      review_failed`) + distinct exit code; blocking (`--wait`) and single-shot status variants.
      `land` is the single **reporter** of the outcome, composing it per the split-provenance
      model above (one bot-state read for `needs_rebase`; live Gerrit reads for
      `ci_failed`/`merged`/`review_failed`) so the agent never correlates primitives itself. The command
      lives in **project tooling** (repo CLI/script), NOT `rebar` core; it may `import rebar` for
      ticket ops.
- [ ] Observability: the bot logs to **its own service logs** (container stdout → existing
      CloudWatch monitoring), NOT `session_log` (which is for agentic sessions only).
- [ ] Docs: rewrite this repo's landing section (`CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md`) to the new
      contract — "set `Autosubmit` / run the land command; act on the single typed outcome; do
      NOT watch votes or treat two green votes as 'it will land'." ADR-0042 (created by S6
      `broken-checkered-tiger`) records the auto-lander + agent contract.
- [ ] **Pre-cutover access baseline (captured by S1 `843b`, BEFORE the `Autosubmit` label is
      pushed to `refs/meta/config`):** save the current `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` response to
      `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` and **commit it in the same change that
      adds the label to `project.config`**, so the ADR-0042 security-invariants diff below has a
      committed baseline to diff against. Without this baseline captured before cutover, the
      access-diff check is unperformable. (S1 owns the capture; S6/ADR-0042 owns the diff.)
- [ ] **Security-invariants artifact:** an ADR-0042 **Security invariants** section enumerating
      each invariant — non-gating `Autosubmit`; requester-votable (`Contributors`) vote ACL, tightly scoped; re-verify `is:submittable` on
      the exact tested SHA immediately before submit; two-vote gate unweakened — and, per
      invariant, the concrete check that proves it (a `grep` of `infra/gerrit/project.config`, a
      `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` diff vs the committed
      `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` baseline, and/or a test asserting the
      re-verify logic) (CVE-2025-1568 / GerriScary posture).

### Phase 2 — throughput (DEFERRED to idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`)
- [ ] No Phase-2 work is in this epic's committed scope. Cross-stack batching was evaluated and
      **rejected** by the design spike (stories S7-S9 archived); the throughput follow-on is
      **bounded speculation**, promoted from the idea only on a measured serial-treadmill pain.

## Explicitly OUT of scope (separate tickets)

- **Cross-stack batching** — evaluated by the Phase-2 design spike and **rejected** on this infra
  (per-patchset CI dispatch → N runs not one; a GerriScary-class `Verified` direct-cast; rare wins +
  bisection tax + WIP-hook fragility). S7-S9 archived; throughput deferred to **bounded speculation**
  (idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`) — the correctly-shaped follow-on, not "speculation ruled out."
- **Platform-agnostic landing provider-seam in `rebar` core** (Gerrit/GitHub/GitLab adapters +
  portable outcome enum) — deferred to a separate `idea` (`1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`; YAGNI until a
  second platform needs to land; consistent with rebar's provider-neutral identity/reconciler
  direction).
compiled_state.title: Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial lander (project infrastructure)
